metadata.archetype_justification: CLI tool and developer framework that generates and deploys IaC to AWS. No persistent state, no production HTTP API, no agent-invoked operations.
metadata.context: Serverless Framework CLI for building and deploying serverless apps.

findings.title: CLI tool — no deployed API interface
findings.description: This is a CLI tool/developer framework, not a deployed API service. The primary interface is the command line (sf-core binary) and the MCP protocol (15 tools via @modelcontextprotocol/sdk). No REST/GraphQL/AsyncAPI production endpoint exists.
findings.gap: No formal API specification exists for the MCP tool surface — tool definitions are in code only.
findings.recommendation: The MCP tool definitions serve as the agent interface. Consider generating documentation from the Zod schemas.

ara_metadata.conditional_resolution: Dev-library-application override applied. No HTTP/RPC surface detected.
ara_metadata.resolution_reasoning: CLI tool with no deployed API surface. The MCP server runs on localhost only for IDE integration.

findings.title: Audit logging — consumer responsibility
findings.description: System does not execute agent-invoked write operations — audit logging is a consumer responsibility. The library/utility is called by applications that own the audit context.
findings.gap: No gap — correct architecture for a dev tool.
findings.recommendation: No action required. Consumers deploying via Serverless Framework inherit CloudTrail audit coverage from their AWS accounts.

ara_metadata.conditional_resolution: Resolved to INFO: agent_scope=read-only AND dev-library-application override (has_auth_surface=false, has_write_operations=false).
ara_metadata.resolution_reasoning: CLI tool does not execute agent-invoked write operations. Audit logging belongs in the AWS account where deployments happen (CloudTrail), not in the deployment tool itself.

findings.title: Identity suspension — consumer responsibility
findings.description: System does not issue or enforce agent identities — suspension is a consumer responsibility. Libraries and utilities are invoked by applications that own identity lifecycle.
findings.gap: No gap — correct architecture for a dev tool.
findings.recommendation: No action required. Agent identity suspension would be managed at the AWS IAM layer.

findings.title: MCP tool schema versioning lacks breaking-change detection
findings.description: The MCP tool definitions use Zod schemas for parameter validation, providing typed tool contracts. The framework is versioned via semver (v4.33.2). CI runs tests on PRs. No formal breaking-change detection exists for MCP tool schemas.
findings.gap: No breaking-change detection for MCP tool parameter schemas.
findings.recommendation: Consider adding schema snapshot tests for MCP tool definitions to detect breaking changes in CI.

findings.title: Sandbox environment — consumer responsibility
findings.description: Library/utility — does not own staging environments. The CI pipeline uses TEST_STAGE for integration tests against a real AWS account. Consumers create their own sandbox environments using the tool's --stage flag.
findings.gap: No gap — correct architecture for a dev tool.

evaluations.rationale: No HTTP/RPC surface — machine-readable spec is not applicable.
No HTTP/RPC surface — structured error responses are not applicable.
agent_scope is read-only — idempotency is informational only.
Dev-library-application override — CLI delegates auth to caller.
Dev-library-application override — scoped permissions enforced by AWS IAM.
Dev-library-application override — action-level auth enforced by AWS IAM.
Extended question; archetype calibration: stateless-utility → INFO.
Dev-library-application override — credentials managed by caller.
Dev-library-application override — not a data-handling target.
No persistent data store — residency not applicable.
No user-data logging — PII risk not applicable.
